2606.20518.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
2606.20518.paper_summary: Flow-matching text-to-speech systems achieve remarkable zero-shot quality but remain static after deployment: pronunciation errors on out-of-vocabulary proper nouns persist unless the model is retrained. We introduce FlowEdit, a life-long adaptation framework for frozen flow-matching TTS that learns pronunciation corrections as latent conditioning edits rather than weight updates. When corrective feedback is provided, FlowEdit optimizes a token-level perturbation in the text embedding space, then stores the correction in a Modern Hopfield Network serving as content-addressable episodic memory. At inference, corrections are retrieved via soft attention with a similarity gate, enabling fuzzy morphological matching. On our curated benchmark of 312 multilingual proper nouns across 18 language families, FlowEdit reduces target-word Phoneme Error Rate by 92.7% relative to the zero-shot baseline while maintaining identical general-speech quality. Corrections complete in approximately 15 seconds on a single GPU.
2606.20518.paper_summary_zh: 流匹配的文本到語音系統在零樣本情況下達到了卓越的質量，但在部署後仍然保持靜態：對於超出詞彙範圍的專有名詞的發音錯誤持續存在，除非模型被重新訓練。我們介紹了FlowEdit，一個針對凍結流匹配TTS的終身適應框架，它學習發音修正作為潛在條件編輯，而不是權重更新。當提供修正反饋時，FlowEdit在文本嵌入空間中優化一個令牌級的擾動，然後將修正存儲在一個作為內容可尋址的情節記憶的現代霍普菲爾德網絡中。在推理時，通過軟注意力和相似性閘檢索修正，實現模糊形態匹配。在我們精心策劃的基準中，涵蓋了18個語言家族的312個多語言專有名詞，FlowEdit相對於零樣本基線將目標詞的音素錯誤率降低了92.7%，同時保持相同的一般語音質量。修正大約在單個GPU上完成，耗時約15秒。
2606.20518.author: Harshit Singh et.al.
2606.20518.authors: Harshit Singh, Ayush Pratap Singh, Nityanand Mathur
